# godozo

> A tiny, self-hostable human-in-the-loop relay. Lets an AI agent (Claude Code,
> any MCP client, or any shell script) **notify** a human and **block for their
> approval** before doing something risky. The human taps Approve/Deny on their
> phone; the agent continues. Outbound-only long-polling — no public webhook, no
> inbound port. Apache-2.0, core + CLI are zero-dependency (Node ≥ 18).

This file is for AI agents asked to "read the docs and wire godozo into this
project." Everything below is real, verified syntax — do not invent flags.

## Two verbs

- `notify(message)` — fire-and-forget ping. Never blocks. Fans out to every
  configured channel (Telegram + Slack).
- `requestApproval({ title, detail })` — BLOCKS until a human taps Approve/Deny
  (or it times out). The return value / exit code IS the decision. Interactive
  approvals run on the primary channel only (Telegram in v0).

## Install & configure

```bash
git clone https://github.com/morganchristian/godozo.git && cd godozo
cp .env.example .env         # fill in Telegram token + chat id
node bin/godozo.js doctor    # prints ✅ primary=telegram bot=@yourbot ...
npm install                  # ONLY if you use the MCP server (adds sdk + zod)
npm link                     # OPTIONAL — lets you type `godozo` instead of `node bin/godozo.js`
```

Telegram setup: message @BotFather → `/newbot` → copy token; message @userinfobot
→ copy numeric chat id; send your bot any message once so it can DM you.

## CLI

```bash
godozo notify <message> [--title T] [--label L]
godozo gate --title T [--detail D] [--timeout SECONDS] [--label L]
godozo gate-hook [--match "substr,substr"] [--title T] [--timeout SECONDS]
godozo listen --exec "<command>" [--timeout SECONDS] | --echo
godozo log [--tail N]
godozo doctor
godozo --help | --version
```

`gate` exit codes: `0` approved · `10` denied · `20` timed out · `1` error — so
it composes: `godozo gate --title "deploy prod?" && ./deploy.sh`.

`gate-hook` is a Claude Code PreToolUse hook. It reads the tool payload as JSON
on stdin and exits `0` to allow / `2` to BLOCK. It fails CLOSED (deny, timeout,
or any error blocks). `--match` narrows to commands containing any listed
substring; without stdin/match it prompts on every matched tool.

## MCP server (the agent's voice)

Exposes two tools — `notify` and `request_approval` — over stdio.

```bash
npm install
claude mcp add godozo -- node /absolute/path/to/godozo/mcp/server.js
```

- `notify({ message, title? })` → sends a ping.
- `request_approval({ title, detail?, timeout_seconds? })` → BLOCKS, returns
  `{ verdict: "APPROVED" | "DENIED" | "TIMED_OUT", by, at }`.

Other clients point at the same `node .../mcp/server.js` (Cursor `.cursor/mcp.json`,
Claude Desktop, Codex, Gemini CLI, VS Code `.vscode/mcp.json`).

MCP is cooperative — the model chooses to call it. For actions that must never
slip, use the deterministic hook below.

## Claude Code hook (the harness's seatbelt)

Add to `.claude/settings.json`. The `matcher` is a tool NAME (e.g. `"Bash"`),
NOT a command pattern. Narrow to specific commands with `gate-hook --match`.

```json
{ "hooks": { "PreToolUse": [
  { "matcher": "Bash",
    "hooks": [{ "type": "command",
      "command": "godozo gate-hook --match \"push --force,push -f,reset --hard,rm -rf\" --title \"Destructive command\"" }] }
]}}
```

Deny on your phone → hook exits 2 → the command is blocked. Because it fails
closed, a missing bot token or lost connection also blocks rather than slips.

## Config (env or ./.env)

| Env | Meaning | Default |
|-----|---------|---------|
| `GODOZO_TELEGRAM_TOKEN` | bot token from @BotFather | required |
| `GODOZO_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID` | your chat id from @userinfobot | required |
| `GODOZO_TELEGRAM_ALLOW` | comma-sep user ids allowed to answer/drive | your chat id |
| `GODOZO_LABEL` | source name shown in messages | `godozo` |
| `GODOZO_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT` | approval wait, seconds | `600` |
| `GODOZO_CHANNEL` | primary channel (approvals/listen) | `telegram` |
| `GODOZO_SLACK_BOT_TOKEN` | Slack bot token (`xoxb-…`) | — |
| `GODOZO_SLACK_CHANNEL` | Slack channel id (`C…`) | — |
| `GODOZO_NOTIFY_CHANNELS` | pin notify fan-out list | all configured |
| `GODOZO_AUDIT` | audit log on/off (`off` disables) | on |
| `GODOZO_AUDIT_FILE` | audit log path | `~/.godozo/audit.jsonl` |

## Notes for agents

- Slack: notify + doctor work; interactive approvals are Telegram-only in v0
  (Slack approvals need Socket Mode — on the roadmap). `gate`/`gate-hook`/`listen`
  use the primary channel.
- One `getUpdates` poller per Telegram bot token: don't run `listen` and a
  `gate` on the SAME token at once (outbound `notify` is always fine).
- Audit: every notify/approval/message appends one JSON line to
  `~/.godozo/audit.jsonl`. Read it with `godozo log --tail N`.
- Files: `src/core.js` (verbs), `src/config.js` (env), `src/channels/*.js`
  (adapters), `bin/godozo.js` (CLI), `mcp/server.js` (MCP). See `DESIGN.md`.
